---
title: "Practical-Exercise"
---

### Text and Images

You can add text here just like a word processor.

## Equations

The equation is $E = mc^2$. Mix of text and Math as, the mean is $\bar{x} = `r mean(c(1,2,3))`$.

## [Quarto](---
title: "My Quarto Analysis"
format: html
---Text and imagesYou can add text here just like a word processor.EquationsThe equation is $E = mc^2$. Mix of text and Math as, The mean of the numbers is $\bar{x} = `r mean(c(1,2,3))`$.Numbered equations$$
E = mc^2
$$Reference it:See Equation @eq-energy.Aligned equationsEquations can align as : $$
\begin{aligned}
y &= mx + b \\
z &= ax^2 + bx + c
\end{aligned}
$$TablesA. Simple Markdown TablesNameAgeScoreJohn2590Mary3095Note: Simple Markdown tables cannot be cross-referenced in Quarto.B. Tables from R (MOST COMMON){r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
# Load libraries and create data
library(knitr)
students <- data.frame(
  Name = c("John", "Mary"),
  Age = c(25, 30),
  Score = c(90, 95)
){r}
#| label: tbl-students
#| tbl-cap: "Student Scores"
   kable(students)Reference as:See Table @tbl-students.Code ChunksCode that appears in your rendered doc{r my-chunk, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}{# This code will appear in the final rendered document} print("Hello Quarto!")Codes that are excluded from the final document{r fig-starwars, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.cap="Relationship between height and mass of Star Wars characters"}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

starwars %>%
  filter(mass < 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mass, y = height, color = gender)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Height vs Mass of Star Wars Characters",
    x = "Mass",
    y = "Height",
    color = "Gender"
  )Code included in TextThe average height of star-wars characters is r round(mean(starwars$height,na.rm=TRUE)/100,2) in meters.Cross ReferencingHere is a reference to @fig-starwarsHow about a call out?remember to use sunscreenAdd a bibliographyHere is some text with citations about R programming .BibliographyQuartoQuarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see https://quarto.org.Running CodeWhen you click the Render button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this: <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD =======Running CodeWhen you click the Render button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:32552b0ea4eed620a5ee07fc0079e6877a14cd50 3bebabe248d9ec6cdc3fde4517d5874ed23d8ed7)

[Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see https://quarto.org.](---
title: "My Quarto Analysis"
format: html
---Text and imagesYou can add text here just like a word processor.EquationsThe equation is $E = mc^2$. Mix of text and Math as, The mean of the numbers is $\bar{x} = `r mean(c(1,2,3))`$.Numbered equations$$
E = mc^2
$$Reference it:See Equation @eq-energy.Aligned equationsEquations can align as : $$
\begin{aligned}
y &= mx + b \\
z &= ax^2 + bx + c
\end{aligned}
$$TablesA. Simple Markdown TablesNameAgeScoreJohn2590Mary3095Note: Simple Markdown tables cannot be cross-referenced in Quarto.B. Tables from R (MOST COMMON){r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
# Load libraries and create data
library(knitr)
students <- data.frame(
  Name = c("John", "Mary"),
  Age = c(25, 30),
  Score = c(90, 95)
){r}
#| label: tbl-students
#| tbl-cap: "Student Scores"
   kable(students)Reference as:See Table @tbl-students.Code ChunksCode that appears in your rendered doc{r my-chunk, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}{# This code will appear in the final rendered document} print("Hello Quarto!")Codes that are excluded from the final document{r fig-starwars, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.cap="Relationship between height and mass of Star Wars characters"}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

starwars %>%
  filter(mass < 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mass, y = height, color = gender)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Height vs Mass of Star Wars Characters",
    x = "Mass",
    y = "Height",
    color = "Gender"
  )Code included in TextThe average height of star-wars characters is r round(mean(starwars$height,na.rm=TRUE)/100,2) in meters.Cross ReferencingHere is a reference to @fig-starwarsHow about a call out?remember to use sunscreenAdd a bibliographyHere is some text with citations about R programming .BibliographyQuartoQuarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see https://quarto.org.Running CodeWhen you click the Render button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this: <<<<<<< HEAD <<<<<<< HEAD =======Running CodeWhen you click the Render button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:32552b0ea4eed620a5ee07fc0079e6877a14cd50 3bebabe248d9ec6cdc3fde4517d5874ed23d8ed7)

```{r}
1 + 1
```
